steps.title: Identify what we need to find
steps.content: We need to find $C(44)$, the y-value when the temperature is $x = 44^{\circ}\text{F}$. This requires locating 44 on the horizontal axis and reading the corresponding chirps per minute from the vertical axis.

steps.title: Read the y-value directly from the graph
steps.content:
Find $x = 44$ on the temperature axis (horizontal). Move vertically upward until reaching the line. Then read horizontally to the left to find the corresponding y-value on the Number of chirps per minute axis.

At $x = 44^{\circ}\text{F}$, the line intersects at $y = 16$.

The estimated number of chirps per minute when the temperature is $44^{\circ}\text{F}$ is 16.
